text: Acme uses PostgreSQL 16 as the primary database for the order service.
Acme runs a managed Postgres 16 cluster on AWS RDS in eu-central-1.
The Acme order service writes about 12 million rows per day to the PostgreSQL orders table.
Acme uses logical replication from PostgreSQL primary to a read replica for the analytics workload.
Acme schedules nightly pg_dump backups of the PostgreSQL order database to S3 at 02:00 UTC.
Acme's Postgres extension pg_partman is used to manage the monthly partitions of the events table.
The Acme analytics warehouse mirrors the PostgreSQL orders schema into ClickHouse for ad-hoc queries.
Acme runs PgBouncer in transaction-pooling mode in front of every PostgreSQL primary to absorb connection bursts.
Acme's Postgres connection pool is sized to 200 backend connections per service, fronted by PgBouncer at 5000 client connections.
Acme upgraded from PostgreSQL 14 to PostgreSQL 16 in Q3 2025 with zero downtime via logical replication.
The Acme catalog service uses a separate PostgreSQL 16 database with the pgvector extension for product embeddings.
Acme stores roughly 4 TB of historical order data in PostgreSQL, split across monthly partitions.
Acme's Postgres tuning sets shared_buffers to 16 GB and effective_cache_size to 48 GB on the order-db instance.
The Acme payments ledger lives in its own PostgreSQL 16 instance to keep the order-db blast radius small.
Acme uses Redis 7 as the session store and short-term cache layer in front of the order service.
Acme's primary API gateway is Kong 3 running in DB-less mode behind an AWS ALB.
Acme runs Kafka 3.6 with three brokers in eu-central-1 for the order-events topic.
Acme's event topic order.placed retains messages for 7 days at the Kafka layer.
Acme's notification service publishes to the order.placed Kafka topic from a Node.js 22 worker pool.
Acme uses Stripe Connect for marketplace payouts to its merchants.
Acme integrates with Auth0 for customer single sign-on and uses JWTs with a 15-minute access-token lifetime.
Acme enforces TOTP-based multi-factor auth for all employee logins to the admin console.
Acme's customer support tool uses Zendesk with custom ticket fields for SLA tracking.
The Acme frontend is a SvelteKit 2 app deployed to Cloudflare Pages with edge caching.
Acme's marketing site uses Astro 5 with the static adapter, served from Cloudflare Pages.
Acme's mobile app is built with React Native 0.76 and ships through Expo's managed workflow.
Acme uses Sentry for client-side error reporting on both web and mobile.
Acme ships infrastructure as Terraform modules tracked in the acme-infra GitHub repo.
Acme runs all production workloads on AWS EKS with managed node groups across three availability zones.
Acme uses ArgoCD for GitOps-style deployments from the acme-platform monorepo to EKS.
Acme's observability stack uses Prometheus, Grafana, and Loki, all hosted on the same EKS cluster.
Acme writes structured JSON logs to stdout and ships them via Vector to Loki.
Acme's CDN strategy puts Cloudflare in front of all customer-facing endpoints with a 60-second edge cache.
Acme uses OpenSearch 2.13 for the customer-facing product search experience.
Acme indexes around 850,000 SKUs into OpenSearch with a nightly full-reindex job.
Acme's recommendation engine runs on a Python 3.12 service using FastAPI and lightgbm.
Acme keeps customer PII in a separate AWS account with stricter IAM boundaries.
Acme's account team owns the relationship with the top 20 retail partners.
Acme's lead source mix in 2025 was 45% organic search, 30% paid social, 25% direct.
Acme uses HubSpot as the CRM for sales pipeline tracking and email sequences.
When the Acme order service shows p99 latency above 800ms, first check the PostgreSQL replication lag dashboard in Grafana.
To run a one-off query against the Acme orders database, port-forward via the bastion host and connect with psql as the readonly role.
To deploy a new version of the Acme order service, push to main and ArgoCD will sync the updated image tag within 90 seconds.
To rotate the Auth0 signing keys for Acme, run the acme-rotate-jwks GitHub Actions workflow against the staging tenant first.
When the Acme Kafka consumer lag for order.placed exceeds 50k messages, scale out the notification-worker deployment.
To refresh the Acme analytics ClickHouse mirror from the Postgres source, trigger the dbt run-warehouse-refresh job.
For Acme incident response, page the on-call SRE via PagerDuty service acme-prod and open a #incident channel in Slack.
To bootstrap a new Acme developer laptop, run the acme-dev-setup script which installs Docker, asdf, and the AWS CLI v2.
For Acme database migrations, write a Sqitch change set and gate it on a manual ArgoCD sync window.
To investigate a stuck Acme order, query the orders table by external_id and join against the payments ledger.
To onboard a new Acme retail partner, create their tenant record via the admin console and seed their default catalog mapping.
When Acme OpenSearch returns stale results, kick the reindex pipeline manually via the acme-search-reindex workflow.
To export an Acme customer's data for a GDPR subject-access request, run the gdpr-export.py script with the customer's external id.
Acme's Q4 2025 onboarding rate hit 142 new merchants per week, ahead of the 120 plan.
The Acme order-service p99 latency is currently around 410ms after the PgBouncer pool resize last week.
Acme is currently running 26 EKS worker nodes, autoscaled between 18 and 40 based on order-service CPU.
Acme migrated 96% of legacy MySQL data into the new PostgreSQL warehouse as of last week.
Acme's monthly AWS spend is around $48k, with EKS and RDS being the two biggest line items.
Acme closed a strategic partnership with Olympia Wholesale for catalog distribution across the DACH region.
Acme's sales team booked $1.4M in net-new ARR in November 2025, the strongest single month of the year.
Acme's customer churn rate dropped to 1.8% in Q4 2025, down from 3.1% in Q3.
Acme's recommendation engine A/B test ended with the lightgbm variant winning by 4.2% conversion lift.
Acme's product team shipped 7 customer-facing features in the last 30 days, double the rolling-quarter average.
Acme finance closed the October 2025 books on time with zero reconciliation gaps.
Acme's incident on 2025-11-03 was caused by a PostgreSQL connection storm after a deploy that bypassed PgBouncer.
Acme learned that ArgoCD diffs become noisy when Helm chart digests are not pinned; pin them to keep PRs reviewable.
Acme found that running EXPLAIN ANALYZE on PostgreSQL queries in production via the readonly replica is the fastest way to triage slow endpoints.
Acme's team learned that batching Kafka consumer commits every 200 messages doubled throughput versus per-message commits.
Acme discovered that the OpenSearch nightly reindex was eating the read capacity; switching to a delta-only update job fixed it.
Acme found that Cloudflare Pages preview deployments are the smoothest review workflow for the SvelteKit frontend.
Acme's SRE team learned that PagerDuty escalation policies need a primary AND a secondary on-call to actually wake someone up at night.
Acme's product team observed that retail partners convert 3x better when their first-week onboarding includes a live demo call.
Acme's marketing learned that LinkedIn ads to retail-operations roles outperformed Facebook 2.3x on cost per qualified lead.
Acme's payments team learned that Stripe Connect onboarding hits friction on French merchants without a SIRET prefilled, so they now prefill it from the registration form.
Acme's RecSys team found that lightgbm beat the Transformer baseline at half the inference cost, so they shipped the gradient-boosted variant first.
Acme's CTO is Maya Lindqvist; she joined from Spotify in early 2024.
Acme's head of growth is Daniel Okafor, reporting directly to the CEO.
Acme's primary office is in Berlin, with a satellite engineering hub in Lisbon since 2024.
Acme is a Series B retail-tech company that raised €45M led by Index Ventures in March 2025.
Acme's core mission is to give independent retailers the same logistics tooling that Amazon-scale operators take for granted.
